findings.title: Screen Reader Navigation Improvement
findings.description: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support for the course catalog and enrollment flow to enable navigation for visually impaired users.
assumptions: Visually impaired users rely on screen readers for navigation.

evidence.excerpt: Screen reader cannot navigate course catalog or enrollment flow.

recommendations: Add ARIA roles to course cards.
Ensure keyboard navigation for all controls.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a visually impaired user WHEN they navigate the course catalog THEN they can access all course cards and complete the enrollment flow using a screen reader.

findings.title: Video Accessibility Features Implementation
findings.description: Add closed captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls to the video player to enhance accessibility for deaf, hard-of-hearing, and motor-impaired users.
assumptions: Users need captions and transcripts for video content.

evidence.excerpt: Video player lacks captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls.

recommendations: Integrate a captioning service.
Ensure all video controls are keyboard operable.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with hearing impairment WHEN they access a video THEN they can view captions and use keyboard controls to navigate the video.

findings.title: Color Contrast Improvements
findings.description: Revise UI components to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios across all text elements to ensure readability for users with visual impairments.
assumptions: Users with visual impairments struggle with low contrast text.

evidence.excerpt: 60% of UI components fail to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios.

recommendations: Audit and adjust color schemes for compliance.
Test contrast ratios for all UI components.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with visual impairments WHEN they view the UI THEN all text elements have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 for normal text.

findings.title: Quiz and Assessment Accessibility Fixes
findings.description: Make quiz and assessment forms accessible to assistive technologies by using semantic HTML elements and providing text descriptions for error messages.
assumptions: Assistive technologies require semantic elements to function properly.

evidence.excerpt: Quiz forms not accessible to assistive technologies.

recommendations: Replace custom controls with semantic HTML elements.
Provide text descriptions for all error messages.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user using a screen reader WHEN they complete a quiz THEN they can navigate all questions and receive error messages with text descriptions.

findings.title: VPAT Creation and Publication
findings.description: Conduct a full accessibility audit, create a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT), and publish an accessibility statement to meet compliance requirements.
assumptions: Clients in regulated sectors require a VPAT for procurement.

evidence.excerpt: Implement VPAT and publish accessibility conformance statement.

recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit.
Draft and publish the VPAT.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a completed accessibility audit WHEN the VPAT is created THEN it accurately reflects the platform's compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

findings.title: User Feedback Collection on Accessibility
findings.description: Implement a mechanism to gather user feedback specifically on accessibility features to inform future improvements.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for understanding accessibility needs.

evidence.excerpt: Insufficient data on user experiences and feedback regarding accessibility features.

recommendations: Create a feedback form focused on accessibility.
Analyze feedback for actionable insights.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the feedback form WHEN they submit feedback THEN it is recorded and categorized for analysis.

findings.title: Accessibility Feature A/B Testing
findings.description: Conduct A/B tests on different accessibility features to measure their impact on task completion rates for users with disabilities.
assumptions: A/B testing can provide insights into effective accessibility features.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct A/B tests on different accessibility features.

recommendations: Design A/B tests for key accessibility features.
Analyze results to inform future development.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN two versions of an accessibility feature WHEN users complete tasks THEN the completion rates are compared to determine effectiveness.

findings.title: Monitoring Accessibility Compliance Metrics
findings.description: Set up monitoring for accessibility compliance metrics to track progress and ensure ongoing adherence to WCAG standards.
assumptions: Continuous monitoring is necessary to maintain compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Tracks the number of critical WCAG violations present on the platform.

recommendations: Implement a dashboard for monitoring compliance metrics.
Regularly review metrics to identify areas for improvement.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN a critical WCAG violation occurs THEN alerts are generated for immediate action.

findings.title: Accessibility Documentation Updates
findings.description: Update documentation to reflect new accessibility features and compliance efforts, ensuring users are informed of changes.
assumptions: Users need clear documentation on accessibility features.

evidence.excerpt: Need to conduct a full accessibility audit, create the VPAT, and publish an accessibility statement.

recommendations: Revise documentation to include new features.
Ensure accessibility statements are clear and accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the documentation is updated WHEN users access it THEN they can find clear information about accessibility features and compliance.

findings.title: Keyboard Navigation for All Components
findings.description: Ensure all interactive components across the platform are operable via keyboard to support motor-impaired users.
assumptions: Motor-impaired users rely on keyboard navigation.

evidence.excerpt: The catalog filter controls are custom dropdowns without keyboard support.

recommendations: Audit all interactive components for keyboard operability.
Implement keyboard navigation for all controls.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a motor-impaired user WHEN they navigate the platform THEN they can access all interactive components using only the keyboard.

findings.title: Error Message Accessibility Improvements
findings.description: Ensure that all error messages are conveyed through both color and text descriptions to support users with visual impairments.
assumptions: Users need clear error messages to understand issues.

evidence.excerpt: Error messages for incorrect answers are conveyed only through color.

recommendations: Provide text descriptions for all error messages.
Ensure color is not the only means of conveying information.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user encounters an error WHEN they view the message THEN it includes both color and text descriptions.

summary: The analysis identified critical accessibility gaps in LearnPath, leading to the creation of a comprehensive set of requirements aimed at enhancing accessibility for visually impaired and motor-disabled users. The requirements cover navigation, video accessibility, color contrast, compliance documentation, and user feedback mechanisms, ensuring a structured approach to addressing these issues.
